title: CA LCFS Quarterly Trend Sheet - Q2 2025
content:
Key Highlights:

In Q2 2025, California LCFS credit generation rose 18.44% quarter-on-quarter to 9.66 MMT, led by strong gains in Bio-CNG (up 33.99% QoQ), Renewable Diesel (up 14.43% QoQ), and EV charging (up 4.78% QoQ), while SAF credits fell 26.39% QoQ. Total reported electricity volumes increased 4.11% QoQ (up 15.68% YoY) to 93.74 million gge, supported by over 20% YoY growth in both residential and non-residential charging. Overall deficits climbed 14.39% QoQ to 6.49 MMT, driven by higher Diesel (up18.51% YoY) and Fossil CNG (up 87.66% YoY) volumes, while CARBOB volumes declined slightly YoY. The average CI of Bio-CNG improved 33.68% QoQ to -211.89 gCOâ‚‚e/MJ, and Renewable Diesel maintained a CI of 42.01 gCOâ‚‚e/MJ. Petroleum displacement under LCFS rose 7.36% QoQ to 1.41 billion gge.
author: Radhika Khandelwal
